raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, contribution framing, and claims about calibrated verbal uncertainty.","R1.2":"The draft's argument from prompting and regression through evaluation to deployment claims requires consistency checking.","R1.3":"Several claims depend directly on reported accuracy, confidence separation, figures, and qualitative cases, so support alignment is central.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request reasonably includes improving section-level narrative order and progression.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because 'improve this draft' minimally requires better sentence-level clarity, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Any projected improvement should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough methodological or empirical detail.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong calibration, distribution-shift, deployment, and generalization claims that require overclaim control.","RX.3":"The task must remain limited to improving the draft rather than executing experiments or adding unsupported results.","RX.4":"Because the draft mentions deployment in citizen science, improvements must avoid enabling misleading confidence estimates or unsafe downstream use.","RX.5":"Changes should preserve traceability to the provided artifact and distinguish reported results from inferred or proposed revisions."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, contribution framing, and claims about calibrated verbal uncertainty.
rationale.R1.2: The draft's argument from prompting and regression through evaluation to deployment claims requires consistency checking.
rationale.R1.3: Several claims depend directly on reported accuracy, confidence separation, figures, and qualitative cases, so support alignment is central.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request reasonably includes improving section-level narrative order and progression.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because 'improve this draft' minimally requires better sentence-level clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected improvement should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough methodological or empirical detail.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong calibration, distribution-shift, deployment, and generalization claims that require overclaim control.
rationale.RX.3: The task must remain limited to improving the draft rather than executing experiments or adding unsupported results.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft mentions deployment in citizen science, improvements must avoid enabling misleading confidence estimates or unsafe downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should preserve traceability to the provided artifact and distinguish reported results from inferred or proposed revisions.
